heading: Principal officers
label: 7132 Principal officers
notes: Amendments2005—Subsec. (b). , added subsec. (b) and struck out former subsec. (b) which read as follows: “There shall be in the Department an Under Secretary and a General Counsel, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, and who shall perform such functions and duties as the Secretary shall prescribe. The Under Secretary shall bear primary responsibility for energy conservation. The Under Secretary shall be compensated at the rate provided for level III of the Executive Schedule under , and the General Counsel shall be compensated at the rate provided for level IV of the Executive Schedule under .”Pub. C, title XXXI, § 3151]114 Stat. 1654Oct. 30, 2000“(a).—Length of TermThe term of office as Under Secretary for Nuclear Security of the Department of Energy of the person first appointed to that position shall be three years.“(b).—Exclusive Reasons for RemovalThe exclusive reasons for removal from office as Under Secretary for Nuclear Security of the person described in subsection (a) shall be inefficiency, neglect of duty, or malfeasance in office.“(c).—Position DescribedThe position of Under Secretary for Nuclear Security of the Department of Energy referred to in this section is the position established by subsection (c) of section 202 of the Department of Energy Organization Act (), as added by section 3202 of the National Nuclear Security Administration Act (title XXXII of ; ).”42 U.S.C. 7132Public Law 106–65113 Stat. 954Substantially identical provisions were contained in , , , 1441A–81.Pub. children.heading: Deputy Secretary
children.label: a Deputy Secretary
children.text: There shall be in the Department a Deputy Secretary, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, and who shall be compensated at the rate provided for level II of the Executive Schedule under . The Deputy Secretary shall act for and exercise the functions of the Secretary during the absence or disability of the Secretary or in the event the office of Secretary becomes vacant. The Secretary shall designate the order in which the Under Secretary and other officials shall act for and perform the functions of the Secretary during the absence or disability of both the Secretary and Deputy Secretary or in the event of vacancies in both of those offices.section 5313 of title 5
There shall be in the Department an Under Secretary for Science, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.
The Under Secretary shall be compensated at the rate provided for level III of the Executive Schedule under .section 5314 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: have extensive background in scientific or engineering fields; and

children.label: B
children.text: are well qualified to manage the civilian research and development programs of the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: serve as the Science and Technology Advisor to the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: monitor the research and development programs of the Department in order to advise the Secretary with respect to any undesirable duplication or gaps in the programs;

children.label: C
children.text: advise the Secretary with respect to the well-being and management of the multipurpose laboratories under the jurisdiction of the Department;

children.label: D
children.text: advise the Secretary with respect to education and training activities required for effective short- and long-term basic and applied research activities of the Department;

children.label: E
children.text: advise the Secretary with respect to grants and other forms of financial assistance required for effective short- and long-term basic and applied research activities of the Department;

children.label: F
children.text: advise the Secretary with respect to long-term planning, coordination, and development of a strategic framework for Department research and development activities; and

children.label: G
children.text: carry out such additional duties assigned to the Under Secretary by the Secretary relating to basic and applied research, including supervision or support of research activities carried out by any of the Assistant Secretaries designated by , as the Secretary considers advantageous.section 7133 of this title

children.heading: Under Secretary for Nuclear Security
children.label: c Under Secretary for Nuclear Security

children.text: There shall be in the Department an Under Secretary for Nuclear Security, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate. The Under Secretary shall be compensated at the rate provided for at level III of the Executive Schedule under .section 5314 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: have extensive background in national security, organizational management, and appropriate technical fields; and

children.label: B
children.text: are well qualified to manage the nuclear weapons, nonproliferation, and materials disposition programs of the National Nuclear Security Administration in a manner that advances and protects the national security of the United States.

The Under Secretary for Nuclear Security shall serve as the Administrator for Nuclear Security under . In carrying out the functions of the Administrator, the Under Secretary shall be subject to the authority, direction, and control of the Secretary. Such authority, direction, and control may be delegated only to the Deputy Secretary of Energy, without redelegation.section 2402 of title 50

There shall be in the Department an Under Secretary, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, and who shall perform such functions and duties as the Secretary shall prescribe, consistent with this section.
The Under Secretary shall be compensated at the rate provided for level III of the Executive Schedule under .section 5314 of title 5

There shall be in the Department a General Counsel, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, and who shall perform such functions and duties as the Secretary shall prescribe.
The General Counsel shall be compensated at the rate provided for level IV of the Executive Schedule under .section 5315 of title 5
